company.foundedNote: Locksmith workshop (Schlossereibetrieb); formal incorporation may be 1951
company.ownership: Family-owned (Böhmer family, 3rd generation succession in progress)
company.employeesNote: ~130 incl. 17 trainees per web research; briefing states ~100
company.revenueConfidence: low estimate — private GmbH, single web source

divisions.name: Steel Fabrication (Systemtechnik)
divisions.description: Contract manufacturing: laser cutting, bending, MIG/MAG-TIG welding, structural steel assemblies

divisions.name: Equestrian Products
divisions.description: Own-brand welded steel equestrian equipment: Heuraufe (hay rack) and Paddockbox (mobile paddock enclosure)

products.name: Heuraufe
products.fp: Store and deliver hay to multiple horses simultaneously in outdoor paddock environments — with all-side access, weather-protected storage, and elevated ground clearance

products.name: Paddockbox
products.fp: Partition and manage outdoor paddock or stable space around horses — with modular reconfigurability, castor-mounted repositionability, and fold-flat post clearance

briefingQuestion: Can Böhmer Systemtechnik — a contract welder with declining order books — successfully launch two own-brand equestrian products (Heuraufe and Paddockbox) into NAICS 112920, and what are the capability gaps, competitive dynamics, and market entry requirements?
briefingAnswer: Both products survive all constraint screening with zero knockouts. Capability-market fit scores 6.71 (Heuraufe) and 6.59 (Paddockbox) — both INVESTIGATE tier. The strongest shared driver is direct customer-facing VN positioning (9.0/10 each). The primary gap preventing PURSUE is market access: no sales channel, no CE documentation, no galvanizer relationship. Critical path: commission hinge fatigue test and qualify external galvanizer in parallel (Month 1-2), then secure 3 pilot customers within 6 months.
